title: Unit 5: Stack
description: Learn about the Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) linear structure and its applications like syntax parsing and evaluation.

topics.title: 5.1 Stack Implementation (Linked List)
topics.description: Implement a LIFO structure where elements are pushed and popped from the head, avoiding capacity constraints.

complexity.time: O(1) for push, pop, and peek

ispo.desc: Accepts integer value to push on stack.
Defines Node class containing data and next pointer in memory.
Declares top reference representing LIFO stack entry point.
Allocates a new Node object in heap memory.
Creates poppedValue variable to hold popped node data value prior to deletion.
Links new node to current top and updates top reference to point to new node.
Validates stack is not empty, reads top data, and shifts top pointer to next node.
Returns popped integer value.
Returns top element data value.

quiz.q: Which acronym describes the insertion and removal ordering of a Stack?

topics.title: 5.2 Balanced Parentheses Checking
topics.description: Use a Stack to check if delimiters like (), [], {} are matched and closed in proper order.

ispo.desc: Receives string equation as argument.
Allocates Stack of Characters to keep track of un-matched opening brackets.
Declares ch variable in memory to hold current character scan.
Declares top variable to hold popped bracket character.
Iterates through string. Pushes open brackets. For closed brackets, pops matching open brackets from stack and verifies correct match.
Returns false immediately if a closing bracket is found without an opening counterpart.
Returns false if popped open bracket doesn't match current closed bracket type.
Returns whether stack is empty (all brackets successfully paired).

quiz.q: What is the state of the stack after processing '[(]'?

topics.title: 5.3 Evaluation of Postfix Expression
topics.description: Evaluate postfix expressions using a stack. Push operands, and when an operator is encountered, pop operands, apply the operator, and push the result.

ispo.desc: Accepts postfix expression string.
Creates Stack of integers to hold operand numbers.
Allocates local variables val1 and val2 to hold the popped operands.
Loops through tokens. If number, pushes it. If operator, pops two values, applies operation and pushes result.
Pops and returns the final evaluated result.

quiz.q: What is the result of evaluating the postfix expression '2 3 1 * + 9 -'?

topics.title: 5.4 Infix to Postfix Conversion
topics.description: Convert infix math expressions (e.g. A + B * C) to postfix notation (e.g. A B C * +) using a stack to buffer operators based on precedence.
topics.cheatSheet:
Precedence: +,- is 1; *,/ is 2; ^ is 3.
Pop operators of higher/equal precedence from stack to output before pushing current operator.

ispo.desc: Accepts infix equation as a string.
Allocates StringBuilder output buffer and operator Character Stack.
Iterates through infix tokens, matching brackets, comparing operator precedence levels, and pushing/popping operators appropriately.
Returns compiled postfix string.

quiz.q: What is the postfix representation of 'A + B * C + D'?

topics.title: 5.5 Reverse a Stack using Recursion
topics.description: Reverse stack elements using recursion call stack without allocating any auxiliary data structures.

ispo.desc: Receives target stack.
Stores current popped value 'temp' in the recursion call stack frame memory.
Stores temp value during the insertAtBottom recursion cycle.
Recursive unwinding. Pops elements to clear the stack, then triggers insertAtBottom to rebuild LIFO in reverse.
Recursive bottom insertion. Pops elements until empty to insert x at base, then pushes items back on top.

quiz.q: What mechanism is used to store elements during stack reversal when no auxiliary data structure is allocated?
